lines.text: Joey and Janine's, Chandler knocks on the front door. Joey answers the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, my name's Chandler. I just moved in next door and I was wondering if you would be interested in battling me in a post-apoplectic world for control of the galaxies last remaining energy source?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, neighbor come on in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, is Janine around?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, no, she's at dance class.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can I check out what she did to my room?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but, hey look, don't go through her stuff. She gets really mad.(Chandler gives him a look and walks to the door of his old room.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (The room is filled with flowers and a floral print sheet on the bed.) Oh my God. What is th... it's like a guy never lived in here. Look, you've got to be careful. This girl thing is dangerous. (Looking around the living room.) It's spreading already.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Looking around the room.) It is???

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Picking up a pillow.) Yeah, is this your pretty pink pillow on the couch?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Pointing to the table and picking up the box.) Is that your tiny little box, that's too small to put anything in?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! Ok, this is not good. You are a guy. Ok? This is a guys place. If you let this go, you're going to be sitting around with your fingers soaking in stuff.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (With Big Eyes.) All right, you're right. I'll talk to her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes talk to her. Be a man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm a man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Defend yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Grunting) Hmm. (Monica opens the front door and comes in.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler come on. We have to hem the new dust ruffle.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Be right there sweetums. (Monica leaves. To Joey.) A totally different situation.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, The gangs all here. Monica is walking in.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, how was your breakfast with Hillary?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It was okay. She's still kind of depressed because she broke up with her boyfriend.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, is this Hillary your HOT assistant chef Hillary?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The one that always stares at me when I come in?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, the one who looked at you once because you got in her way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Still I could tell. She was into me. (Joey rolls his eyes.) Well, why don't you set us up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, are you setting Ross up with someone? Does she have a wedding dress?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Walking over and Sitting down.) Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm, uhh, making up flyers trying to get new massage clients. So, can I come to Bloomingdale's and use the copy machine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, sure, but they might think it's kinda weird considering I don't work there anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God. What happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I, got a job at Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well that's great! Congratulations!! (She hugs Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (chuckling) A year ago..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Hugging her again.) You've lasted a whole year. Good for you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But, Pheebs, you can still use the copy machine where I actually work. But, just come by at lunch so my boss doesn't see you. Cause Kim will just freak out and she already doesn't like me very much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's weird. I don't think my boss likes me either.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't think mine likes me either.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe it's a universal thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or maybe, it's because you're hanging around here at 11:30 on a Wednesday. (Everybody gets up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah let's head off to work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We should go.
Rachel's office, Phoebe hands Rachel a key card.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now you will not believe this. But, I was in the copy room, making copies, and Ralph Lauren came in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God. Did you talk to him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah a little. He seems really nice. Good kisser.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What!?! You kissed him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Totally.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Gasps) Phoebe are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. I was just in there. He introduced himself and the next thing I know, we're making out. You know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, I mean, you do know he's married?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What am I supposed to do? Ask every guy I make out with if he's married? (Rachel looks at her.) No, yeah, I should.
Joey and Janine's apartment, Joey stares at a picture of a bay on the wall. Janine comes out of her room.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hey Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. Uh, can I talk to you for a second? This, uh, kid in this picture. Do you, uh, know this kid? Is that like a relative or something?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: No, I just thought it was cute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, that's what I was afraid of.. Okay, uhh...Look Janine I really want you to feel at home here, but some of this new stuff. It's...too girly.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Ohh. Like what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like this. (pointing to the picture) Pictures of cute babies we don't know. We..we can't have that.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey, it's Anne Geddes. She's a famous artist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look I don't know this baby. I don't know if she's a famous artist or not. You know, and I don't want to be a jerk but you're changing too much around here.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry. I just thought I'd try to make the place a little nicer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah but it's too much stuff. You know like, you got the candles and the foofy schmoofer thing here and over here you got a picture of a watering can.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well I just thought...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sure it's a famous watering can, okay. But, come on...and what is with the really hot stick in the bathroom?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: It's a curling iron.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, well, that's ok then. But, okay my towels for instance. I come in to the bathroom here and my towel is not on the floor where keep it. It's up here on some hook..and...smells different.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: It's clean.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, well, it feels different.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: It's dry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, I can make my peace with the clean dry towels...Also what is with these chips you bought?

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: No no no no, it's potpourri. You're supposed to smell it. (Joey takes a big whiff of the potpourri.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Voice cracking) Well that's like summer in a bowl.
Elevator at the Ralph Lauren offices, Rachel gets on her boss Kim is there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Kim, Hi. (Kim doesn't even look up from her report.)

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Uhh-huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So you know, I...I handed in that marketing report and I never got to hear what you thought.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: I didn't read it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ahh....So...Wow...The spring line, it's really going to be great this year, huh?

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So I hear the Ralph Lauren fooled around with someone in the copy room. (Kim stops the elevator and turns to Rachel.)

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Tell me everything.
; Central Perk, Monica and Chandler are on the couch. Ross walks in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's up? (He smiles. His teeth are freakishly white.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know...Oh My God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What happened to your teeth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I whitened them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Sarcastically) Really.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. What do you think.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I think I shouldn't look directly at them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, seriously.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross they're really, really, really white.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, what was wrong with your old...human teeth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ahh, I-I did leave the gel on a little longer then it said to.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How much longer?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A-A day.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross you know that tonight is your date with Hillary?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. That's why I did it. (With a big smile) Come on, are they really that bad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no no no. You'll be fine. (turning to Monica) Hillary's bind, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She will be after tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. (Rachel walks in.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, hey, hey Rach, do you notice anything..ahh...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Your teeth? Yes, I saw them from outside. (Sitting down on the couch.) You guys are never going to believe this. But, Phoebe made out with Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What???

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know. She ran into him at my office and they just...made out. And the craziest thing is, now my boss likes me because I told her about it and she said it was the best gossip she'd heard all year.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am proud of all my friends today.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My God, Rachel, I can't believe Phoebe made out with Ralph Lauren. Ohh, I'm so jealous. (Chandler looks at her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, I'm Chandler. Your live-in boy

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, please, come on. Look at him. (Pointing to a picture of Ralph on a magazine,.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I am no women, but that is one tasty dish. (Phoebe walks in.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey. Here. (Hands Chandler a copy of her flyer and sees the picture of Ralph.) Ohh, who's the silver fox?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That-that is your make out buddy. Don't you recognize him? (Holding up the magazine in front of her face.) Oh wait. Ohh, Phoebe I love you. Kiss me please.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's not Ralph Lauren. Sounds like him though.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, no, Ralph doesn't look anything like that guy. He's-he's young and he's got long hair and a beard and a hacky sack.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh My God, Phoebe, that's not Ralph Lauren. That's Kenny the copy guy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh..Go..Oh..and I told my boss that someone made out with Ralph Lauren. If she finds out that I lied to her, she is going to hate me even more. Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why would the copy guy say he was Ralph Lauren?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: To get you to make out with him!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh.
Ross's Apartment, Ross and Monica are there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Holding a shirt in front of Ross.) Okay, maybe this will make your teeth look less white. (Ross has a big smile.) Nope. Okay, colors that don't work are blue, yellow, green, red, black, white, orange, and purple.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know what I'm going to do. That date starts in like an hour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Ross, maybe if your skin was lighter. Your teeth wouldn't look so bright.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great. So all I need to do is get some new skin. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm just saying, if we put just a little bit of makeup on you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa. Whoa. Whoa. We're not 13 anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross this is the only thing left that has a shot at working.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But, won't she notice I have makeup on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please. Half the guys out there have makeup on.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What??

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, half the people. I mean, just try it and see.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. I am not putting on makeup. (Knock at the door. Phoebe enters.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello. Oh good. Ross could you put up some of these flyers for me? (He smiles at her.) OH!! Demon!! Demon!!
Chandler's and Monica's apartment, Chandler and Monica are sitting at the kitchen table making potpourri sachets.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now are drawers will smell nice and we didn't waste these pantyhose.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, God forbid we throw out old underwear. You-you know what? I'm going to go over to Joey's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, we're supposed to organize the wrapping paper drawer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but I feel like I've really gotten in touch with my feminine side enough today. You know. In fact I think we're two sachets away from becoming a lesbian couple.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what? This has been kind of a girlie day. You're right, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nah, Nah, it's okay. I feel like I need to be in guy place. You know, do kind of like a man thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. Go over to Joey's. Go over to Joey's and drink some beer and hammer up some drywall.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know when guys hang out they don't just drink some beer and hammer up drywall?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When girls hang out, we don't have pillow fights in our underwear. (Chandler gets a hurt look on his face.) I'm sorry. We do. We do. I don't know why I said that.
Joey and Janine's apartment, Chandler walks in. Joey and Janine are knitting at the kitchen counter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Chandler. Come on in. We're knitting pot holders.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No thanks, Josephine.
Ross's apartment, Chandler enters. Ross is putting on makeup.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Ross, I was wondering if... Oh my God!! Where are all the men???
Elevator at the Ralph Lauren offices, Kim's waiting for it. The door opens and Rachel is inside.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, hi, Kim.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Hi Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, remember that thing I told you that happened yesterday? Well it didn't happen.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: You didn't cancel the fabric order from Taiwan?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, two things didn't happen. Remember I told you that someone made out with Ralph Lauren in the copy room? Well, it turns out that's not true.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: That's not true?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Oh that's interesting? Because I checked and only one keycard was used to access the copy machine yesterday during lunch and that keycard belonged to you, Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, no, no. Oh God, you think I made out with him.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Listen to me. If you think sleeping with Ralph is going to get you my job. You are sadly mistaken.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I don't want your job. I-I don't. Ohh this is such a mistake. I did not make out with him. Nobody made out with him. I did not use my keycard yesterday. I don't even know how to use my keycard. (The elevator stops. Ralph steps on.)

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Hi Ralph.
Ralph Lauren:: Hi Kim. (Dead silence until Ralph gets off the elevator.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Yeah, nothing happen. You could cut the sexual tension in here with a knife.
Hillary's apartmen,. Hillary and Ross are having dinner. He is avoiding opening his mouth.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: And after that, what could I do except become a chef.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mmm-Hmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: And someday soon, I hope to open my own restaurant.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: You know, you're a really great listener. Most guys I go out with, they just talk and talk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mm-Hmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: After a while it's like, shut your mouth, you know?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Chuckling) Hmm-Hmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: I've probably been talking too much. Why don't we talk about you a little bit?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mmm-Unmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Come on. I want to know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm-Hmm-Hmm. (Putting his hand up to block his mouth.) Okay. I, uh, am from Long Island. I-I came to the city for college. Um, I, um, have a 5 year old son and in my spare time I like to read spy novels. But, but, let's talk more about you. Hmm.
Joey and Janine's apartment, Joey and Monica are arranging flowers. Chandler walks in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what's really neat. If you sear the stems of the flowers first in a frying pan, your arrangement will look fresh much longer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, Joey, that is such a great tip.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, could you excuse us for a second? I need to talk to the girl with the flowers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Oh but Joey, come over later because I'm going to teach you to make a bird feeder out of just a pine cone and some peanut butter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, I love birds. (Monica leaves. Chandler shuts the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is the matter with you ?!?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're arranging flowers! (Pointing to the dish on the table.) You got dead flowers! You got a picture, a picture, of a baby dressed like flowers! This is not Joey!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey. Hey look I am still Joey, okay. Flowers they're just, you know, they're nice to look at. And that happens to be a picture by a famous artist. Of a famous baby.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're turning into a women.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No I'm not. Why would you say that? That's just mean.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now I've upset you? What did I say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's not what you said. It's the way you said it....Oh My God, I'm a women!!!
Central Perk, Phoebe and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now, she thinks that I made out with him and I did it to get her job.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But why didn't you just tell her the truth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I did but she doesn't think anyone would be stupid enough to confuse Kenny the copy guy with Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, hey, what if Kenny were the real brains behind the whole company? You know. What if Kenny hired that Ralph Lauren guy to be the pretty front man? Huh, did she ever think of that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You were with Kenny today, weren't you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Just for a second.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, Phoebe, what am I going to do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, the only thing you can do. Sleep with Ralph Lauren.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm not going to sleep with Ralph Lauren. I mean, I could, but I wouldn't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, sleep with Kenny.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That wouldn't help me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, yes it would.
Hillary's apartment, Hillary and Ross are finishing up their date.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: I've had a really good time tonight.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: You know, I rarely connect with someone this much on the first date.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Giggling) Hmm-Hmm-Hmm. (Picking up a piece of bread and covering his mouth.) Me, neither. I've had a really good time too, you know. (Putting the bread down.)

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Are you going to eat that bread?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Putting the bread up to his nose.) Ohh, I just like the smell. (Sniffing it.) Mmmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: (Laughing) You make me laugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm-Hmm.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Would you like to move over to the couch?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mmm-Hmm. (Picking up their wine glasses.)

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Maybe I'll just turn the lights down a little.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Covering his mouth with the glass.) How about all the way.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Okay. (She goes to turn the lights off and Ross sits on the couch. She has some black light posters on the wall.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Looking at the posters.) Wow, cool poster. Or should I say groovy poster? (They sit down on the couch. Ross smiles and the black light fluoresces his teeth.) So, ahh, where were we?

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Are those your teeth??

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh, you can see them, huh?

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: Yes. They're insanely white.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I, did that for you.

lines.speaker: Hillary
lines.text: What's a matter with you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's a matter with me? You've got a black light. It's 1999!
Elevator at the Ralph Lauren Offices, Rachel gets in . Kim is there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Kim, hi.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Hi Rachel. Ohh, I've been meaning to ask you. Have you seen the new Ralph Lauren sheets? Ohh, what am I thinking. Of course you have.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay..Okay.. Look. I'm sorry that I lied to you before. You were right. Ralph and I were an item but were not anymore.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Oh, really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Faking crying.) Yeah, he dumped me. He said, "Rachel, I can't do this. Even though you are a very, very, very beautiful women. I can't do this. I'm married and I'm sorry." And then I don't know why but he said, "and you will never get promoted. Especially not above Kim, who is an integral cog in the Ralph Lauren machine."

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: You expect me to believe..(Doors open, Ralph steps in.) Hi Ralph.
Ralph Lauren:: Hi Kim. (Dead silence again until he gets off.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Oh my God. He just gave you the coldest look I have ever seen. It's like he hates you. Then it is true.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course it's true and it hurts so bad.

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Ohh honey come here. (Hugging Rachel.) Ohh it will be ok. We've all been there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You and Ralph?

lines.speaker: Kim
lines.text: Kenny the copyboy.
Ending Credits
Joey and Janine's apartment, Joey paces the floor waiting for Janine. She walks in.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, uhh, I need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: What's the matter? Are you upset?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sorry but we've got to get rid of all this girlie stuff in here. I, uh, I got to be a man! Okay. The living room has to remain a guy place, okay? That's just the way it has to be.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Well, if that's what you want. I'll just put it all in my room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Great...Great...and thanks for being so understanding. I mean, I didn't want to make a big deal out of this, you know. (She starts to collect all the girlie stuff up.) You could, uh, put the picture of the famous baby in my room. I mean, if you want to.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And, uhh, maybe the watering can there.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And a couple of these little tiny boxes.

lines.speaker: Janine
lines.text: Joey? Do you want me to put it all in your room?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Smiling) Okay.
End
